confirmed_conclusion: Within AHV model: TLS with E_TLS >= 10 ueV are frozen at 15mK and fully active at room temperature.
critical_caveat: Room temperature DIET may remove TLS with E_TLS >= 10 ueV (active at RT), but qubits at 15mK are primarily affected by TLS with E_TLS < 1.3 ueV. Whether RT DIET selectively removes the relevant TLS is unknown.
limitations: Actual E_TLS distribution of TLS on Au nanogap is unmeasured
Which energy range DIET preferentially removes is unknown
AHV model applicability to Au nanogap TLS is unverified
de Graaf 2018 desorption energy ~8600 ueV is ~6600x larger than kT(15mK)=1.3 ueV. Direct connection between DIET and qubit-relevant TLS removal is unverified.

de_Graaf_2018_energy_scale.source: de Graaf et al., Nat. Commun. 9, 1143 (2018), Discussion section
de_Graaf_2018_energy_scale.quote: For weakly absorbed spins it is reasonable to expect that W ~ 100 K, limited by the observed desorption energy.

energy_gap_problem.conclusion: The TLS removed by DIET in de Graaf 2018 (desorption energy ~8600 ueV) are at an energy scale ~6600x larger than TLS affecting qubits at 15mK (E < 1.3 ueV). Whether DIET removes the TLS relevant to qubit operation is an open experimental question.
energy_gap_problem.significance: This energy scale discrepancy is a previously unquantified problem. Identifying it is a novel theoretical contribution of this work.

material_caveat.implication: AHV model calculations in this work use E_TLS ranges inferred from general TLS theory. The actual E_TLS distribution on Au nanogap surfaces is unknown. Sarabi 2016 d_TLS values (SiNx) cannot be directly applied to Au nanogap TLS without experimental verification.
